Title: Comparative single-nucleus RNA-seq analysis revealed localized and cell type-specific pathways governing root microbiome interactions

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Translation regulator; ATP-grasp domain-containing protein involved in eIF2 complex assembly
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for beneficial WCS417-mediated root growth promotion; mutant blocks increase in lateral root number and shoot fresh weight
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (cdc123/dst7); WCS417 inoculation growth assay; lateral root counting; shoot fresh weight measurement; colonization assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not cell type-specific; implicated in proximal meristem-associated translational response
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: translation initiation factors (13 reported interactors in prior studies)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to maintain or enhance beneficial microbe-mediated growth promotion, but broad translational function suggests pleiotropic risk
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic developmental effects due to central role in translation initiation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified here as an upstream translational regulator necessary for a beneficial microbe response rather than as a classic metabolism enzyme or TF
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDC123 is necessary for WCS417-mediated plant growth promotion. 2. cdc123 mutant blocks lateral root and shoot growth promotion without significantly affecting WCS417 colonization. 3. The study links translational control to compatible beneficial root-microbe interaction.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Translation regulator; organizer of translation-related components
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for beneficial WCS417-mediated root growth promotion; mutant blocks increase in lateral root number and shoot fresh weight
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (hem1/dst5); WCS417 inoculation growth assay; lateral root counting; shoot fresh weight measurement; colonization assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not cell type-specific; function associated with proximal meristem translational program
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 35 cytosolic ribosome translation initiation, elongation, or release factors (reported previously)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering beneficial microbe responsiveness, but likely unsuitable for simple manipulation because of broad translational role
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects expected from master translation regulator
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity described as post-translationally regulated by phase separation into condensates with translation components
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HEM1 is required for WCS417-mediated growth promotion. 2. hem1 mutant impairs lateral root and shoot biomass responses despite normal WCS417 colonization. 3. Translational regulation is causally linked to beneficial microbe-induced host growth.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly initiates biosynthesis of triterpene products that reshape the root microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway gene; exact rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Shapes root microbiome composition during pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root maturation zone, especially cortex and atrichoblast-associated expression domains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for reshaping the root microbiome upon Ralstonia GMI1000 infection; thas1 mutant fails to significantly alter microbiome structure after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq expression profiling; pTHAS1::YFP-NLS reporter validation; thas1 mutant microbiome sequencing in natural soil; PCoA and differential abundance analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, especially cortex and atrichoblast; weak in meristem/elongation zones; not induced by WCS417
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ralstonia solanacearum GMI1000 infection; natural soil microbiome context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No effect on Ralstonia resistance in mono-association assay; impacts microbiome structure rather than direct resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering pathogen-triggered microbiome reshaping to recruit protective microbes; should be considered in microbiome-informed breeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: thas1 mutant blocked enrichment of potentially protective Oxalobacteraceae upon GMI1000 infection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAS1 is specifically induced by GMI1000 in root maturation-zone outer cell types. 2. Reporter analysis confirms localized induction in cortex and atrichoblast cells. 3. thas1 blocks pathogen-triggered root microbiome reshaping, linking triterpene biosynthesis to microbiome regulation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms downstream intermediate in triterpene products implicated in microbiome reshaping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with root microbiome sculpting during pathogen response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by GMI1000 in maturation cell types, suggesting involvement in pathogen-responsive triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, higher in cortex and atrichoblast than endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for pathway stacking with THAS1 to modulate pathogen-responsive triterpene output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of four thalianin biosynthesis genes with localized induction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAH is one of the triterpene biosynthetic genes strongly induced by GMI1000. 2. Its expression is enriched in maturation-zone outer root cells. 3. It supports the conclusion that pathogen-responsive triterpene biosynthesis is spatially localized.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of mature triterpene product pool
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with root microbiome sculpting during pathogen response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, implicating it in localized triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, especially cortex and atrichoblast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary candidate for engineering full thalianin pathway output in pathogen-triggered contexts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment inferred from pathway naming in figure/text; direct biochemical assay not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAR1 shows pathogen-specific localized induction in maturation cells. 2. It is part of the triterpene biosynthetic module activated by GMI1000. 3. Its expression pattern supports localized microbiome-shaping metabolism in outer root tissues.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for mature triterpene pathway completion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with root microbiome sculpting during pathogen response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, implicating it in localized triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by GMI1000 in maturation cells, especially cortex and atrichoblast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary candidate for engineering pathogen-responsive triterpene biosynthesis together with THAS1 branch genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment inferred from pathway context in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THAR2 is part of the GMI1000-induced triterpene biosynthetic module. 2. Its induction is localized to maturation-zone root cells. 3. It supports localized production of microbiome-shaping triterpenes during pathogen interaction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a key oxidation step in camalexin biosynthesis from tryptophan-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a key intermediate toward camalexin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical biosynthetic gene; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root maturation zone of atrichoblast, cortex, and endodermis under pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong pathogen-induced expression in maturation zone indicates localized camalexin biosynthesis during GMI1000 infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: snRNA-seq expression profiling; pCYP71A12::YFP-NLS reporter validation; qRT-PCR marker analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very specifically induced in maturation zone of atrichoblast, cortex, and endodermis upon GMI1000 treatment; weak in meristem zone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ralstonia solanacearum GMI1000 infection; 6 h treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker and candidate for engineering localized root antimicrobial metabolism against soil-borne pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a key experimental reporter to validate developmental stage-specific immune-metabolic activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71A12 is specifically induced by GMI1000 in root maturation-zone cells. 2. Reporter imaging validates stronger pathogen response in maturation versus meristem zone. 3. The gene serves as a core marker of localized antimicrobial metabolite biosynthesis.
